---
title: "Porting Status"
---

# Porting Status

The grimoire source corpus has been ported into the public Quarto site and repository.

| Source | Ported form | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `software_magic_grimoire_v3_public_release.docx` | Main book chapters, public canon, full lexicon data, generated reference pages | Structurally ported; lexicon authoring in progress |
| `pocket_grimoire_software_spellcraft_final.docx` | Pocket field guide, 300-rune pocket canon, quick-reference pages | Ported |
| `software_spellcraft_addendum_on_stacked_spells.docx` | Stackcraft chapter, stack grammar, six generated stack pages | Ported |

The first public seed was a complete structural port. The reader-linking pass improved the reading layer: guided paths, rune anchors, term index links, spell-to-rune links, stack-to-spell links, and canon maps. The current integrity layer is honest about lexicon authoring state.

## Lexicon Completion

The master lexicon currently has `305` authored entries and `1302` stub entries.

| Status      | Count |
| ----------- | ----- |
| authored    | 305   |
| stub        | 1302  |
| needs_sense | 38    |

## Authored-Layer Quality

| Measure                            | Value   |
| ---------------------------------- | ------- |
| Authored entries                   | 305     |
| Stub entries                       | 1302    |
| Authored unique summaries          | 305     |
| Authored unique shadows            | 121     |
| Doubled shadow labels              | 0       |
| Major-canon reviewed shadows       | 50/50   |
| Major-canon unique shadows         | 50      |
| Overloaded entries with real sense | 336/336 |

## Completion by House

| House                                                          | Authored | Stub | Needs Shadow | Needs Sense |
| -------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ---- | ------------ | ----------- |
| Architecture, Abstraction, and Design                          | 21       | 87   | 0            | 4           |
| Language, Semantics, and Formal Shape                          | 20       | 114  | 0            | 3           |
| Data, State, and Representation                                | 18       | 103  | 0            | 1           |
| Transformation, Algorithms, and Working Verbs                  | 21       | 112  | 0            | 7           |
| Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | 18       | 78   | 0            | 1           |
| Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | 14       | 60   | 0            | 2           |
| Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | 12       | 73   | 0            | 1           |
| Networking and Distributed Systems                             | 21       | 90   | 0            | 5           |
| Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | 16       | 66   | 0            | 0           |
| Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | 18       | 76   | 0            | 1           |
| Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | 16       | 59   | 0            | 1           |
| Testing, Verification, and Observability                       | 16       | 54   | 0            | 1           |
| Hardware, Embedded, and Performance-Near Words                 | 15       | 65   | 0            | 0           |
| Interface, UX, and Human-Facing Words                          | 10       | 50   | 0            | 0           |
| Promptcraft, AI-Oriented Engineering, and Spell Structure      | 14       | 48   | 0            | 8           |
| Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | 19       | 70   | 0            | 3           |
| Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | 20       | 50   | 0            | 0           |
| Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | 16       | 47   | 0            | 0           |

## What Counts As Complete

- Source text is preserved.
- Every lexicon entry has a stable sigil, structured data record, generated reference page location, and direct anchor.
- Major and pocket canon entries link into the master house pages.
- Spell templates link to relevant runes and supporting reference pages.
- Stack workflows link to relevant spell templates, runes, and stack grammar.
- Stub lexicon entries are explicitly marked as stubs until they receive term-specific summaries, shadows, and sense disambiguation.
- The roadmap distinguishes archival completeness from reader experience completeness.
